howItWorks.intro: This football qb rating calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: NFL Passer Rating
rules.explanation: Four components: completion%, yards/att, TD%, INT%.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a perfect passer rating?
faq.answer: 158.3 is the maximum NFL passer rating.

faq.question: What is an average QB rating?
faq.answer: Around 85-90 is league average in the modern NFL.

faq.question: Is the Football QB Rating Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard sports analytics formulas trusted by athletes, coaches, and fans. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What completions should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate completions value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Football QB Rating Calculator?
sections.body: The Football QB Rating Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes, coaches, and fans who need quick, accurate calculations in the sports analytics space. By entering your completions, attempts, passing yards, you get instant results including passer rating, grade. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting passer rating right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In sports analytics, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes, coaches, and fans worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to analyze performance with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your completions and need to find the right passer rating. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Completions
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your completions — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: attempts, passing yards, touchdowns, interceptions. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your passer rating, grade. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your completions before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both passer rating and grade — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to analyze performance.
If you're unsure about your interceptions, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
